characters.name: Ebenezer Scrooge
motivations: later: redeem himself and help others

relationships.description: Dead business partner who warns Scrooge from beyond the grave
Cratchit's sick son whose fate becomes Scrooge's catalyst for change
Nephew who persistently offers love despite Scrooge's rejection

characters.name: Jacob Marley's Ghost
motivations: save Scrooge from his own fate

relationships.description: Former partner, now warning from beyond death

characters.name: Ghost of Christmas Past
motivations: show Scrooge what he has lost

relationships.description: Forces Scrooge to confront his own history

characters.name: Ghost of Christmas Present
motivations: show Scrooge the joy and suffering happening now

relationships.description: Shows Scrooge the world he has shut out

characters.name: Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come
motivations: show Scrooge his death and legacy if unchanged

relationships.description: Embodies the fate Scrooge must choose to avoid
Beloved son whose life hangs in the balance
Keeps inviting Scrooge to Christmas dinner despite years of rejection

places.name: Scrooge & Marley's Counting House
places.description: A cold, dark office where Scrooge hoards money and denies warmth to his clerk.

places.name: Scrooge's Bedroom
places.description: The sparse, cold chamber where Scrooge is visited by spirits — the stage of his ordeal.

places.name: The Cratchit Home
places.description: A humble but warm home full of love, meager food, and Tiny Tim's infectious spirit.

places.name: The Graveyard
places.description: Where Scrooge sees his own neglected grave — the ultimate crossroads between death and redemption.

places.name: London on Christmas Morning
places.description: The same city Scrooge has lived in for decades, now radiant with the warmth he finally allows himself to feel.

objects.name: Marley's Chains
objects.description: Heavy chains forged from Marley's lifetime of greed — each link a selfish act.
objects.significance: Shows Scrooge the price of his current path. Scrooge's own chains are longer.

objects.name: Tiny Tim's Crutch
objects.description: The small crutch propped by an empty chair — signaling Tim's death in the unchanged future.
objects.significance: The most emotionally devastating symbol. Its presence or absence marks the future Scrooge chooses.
rules: Present in the future vision = Tim has died
Absent in the redeemed timeline = Tim lives

objects.name: Scrooge's Gravestone
objects.description: A neglected headstone bearing Scrooge's name — unmourned, uncelebrated.
objects.significance: The catalyst for Scrooge's final transformation. Seeing his own forgotten death breaks him.
rules: Appears only in the Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come's vision

moments.label: Bah, Humbug
moments.description: Scrooge is a cold, miserly man who dismisses Christmas, charity, and human connection.

char_scrooge.wants: to be left alone with his money
knows: nothing about his own spiritual poverty

moments.label: Marley's Ghost
moments.description: Jacob Marley's ghost appears in chains, warning Scrooge that three spirits will visit him.

transitions.detail: Marley is damned by his greed, and Scrooge's chain is even longer

moments.label: Ghost of Christmas Past
moments.description: The first spirit shows Scrooge his lonely boyhood, his lost love Belle, and the warmth he once had.

transitions.detail: He was once capable of love and was loved in return

transitions.description: His vulnerability breaks through — he weeps for what he lost

moments.label: Christmas Present and Future
moments.description: The second spirit shows the Cratchits' humble joy and Tiny Tim's fragility. The third shows Scrooge's unlamented death and Tim's empty chair.

transitions.detail: Tiny Tim will die without help. Scrooge himself will die unmourned.

transitions.description: Confronts the full truth of his wasted life at his own gravestone

moments.label: The Gravestone Promise
moments.description: Kneeling at his own grave, Scrooge begs for the chance to change. He swears he will honor Christmas.

transitions.description: Swears to change his life — 'I will honor Christmas in my heart'

moments.label: Christmas Morning Transformation
moments.description: Scrooge wakes transformed. He sends a giant turkey to the Cratchits, gives money to charity, and raises Bob's salary.

transitions.description: Becomes generous, warm, and joyful — the opposite of his former self
Symbolically breaks the chains of greed through acts of generosity

moments.label: A New Scrooge
moments.description: Scrooge becomes a second father to Tiny Tim, who does not die. He keeps Christmas well forever after.

transitions.description: Becomes a second father to Tiny Tim
Accepts his nephew's love and joins his family
Permanent renewal — 'he knew how to keep Christmas well, if any man alive possessed the knowledge'
